title: pear tart bourdaloue
description: A great French classic pear tart with almond cream in a crisp pastry case.
context: Pâtisserie course 2012 – The Raymond Blanc Cookery School. The pastry is enough to line two pastry cases; it can be prepared in advance and frozen. You can prepare this entire dessert up to 1 day before serving.

ingredients.name: Poire William or other eau de vie (optional)

steps: In a large bowl, rub together the flour, sugar, butter and salt using your fingertips until sandy in texture. Create a well in the centre and add the egg. Work the flour and butter mixture into the egg, then press together to form a ball. Lightly flour your work surface and knead with the palms of your hands until consistently blended, for a maximum of 30 seconds. Wrap in cling film and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Combine all the almond cream ingredients in a bowl and mix until well blended. Reserve.

Roll the pastry to a thickness of 2 mm. Line a 21 cm x 2.5 cm tart ring with the pastry. Place on a tray lined with greaseproof paper and refrigerate for 1 hour to relax the pastry and prevent shrinkage during cooking.
Spread the almond cream on the bottom of the pastry case. Arrange the pear halves attractively on top of the cream. Sprinkle caster sugar over the tart.
Bake in the hottest part of the oven for 45 minutes, until the pastry and topping are golden in colour. Remove from the oven and allow to cool.

Run the blade of a knife around the inside of the ring to gently separate the tart, then lift the ring off. Place on a cooling rack, transfer to a serving tray, sprinkle the edges with a little icing sugar, and serve.
